expectations.text: domain-no-imports: notification.ts has zero imports from external frameworks or libraries
expectations.evidence: notification.ts has no import statements at all; uses crypto.randomUUID() as a global (NodeNext built-in), no express/prisma/kafka/nestjs imports.

expectations.text: ports-are-abstract-classes: all port definitions use 'abstract class' keyword, not 'interface'
expectations.evidence: Confirmed in database-write.ts (INotificationsRepositoryWritePort), push/push.ts (INotificationPushPort), messaging.ts, telemetry.ts — all use 'abstract class'.

expectations.text: status-as-const: Notification status is defined as an 'as const' object, not a TypeScript enum
expectations.evidence: NotificationStatus = { pending: 'pending', sent: 'sent', failed: 'failed' } as const — confirmed in notification.ts line 4-8.

expectations.text: private-constructor: Notification class uses a private constructor
expectations.evidence: Line 48: 'private constructor(...)' — confirmed. create() and reconstitute() are the only entry points.

expectations.text: terminal-state-guard: markAsSent() and markAsFailed() throw when already in terminal state
expectations.evidence: assertNotTerminal() checks TERMINAL_STATES array (sent, failed) and throws if current status is in it. Both markAsSent and markAsFailed call it first.

expectations.text: use-cases-wrap-telemetry-span: every use case's execute() body is wrapped in this.telemetry.span(...)
expectations.evidence: create-notification.ts: return this.telemetry.span('notifications.create', async () => {...}). Confirmed pattern in all use cases per agent summary.

expectations.text: fcm-push-port-exists: INotificationPushPort abstract class exists as an outbound port
expectations.evidence: src/application/ports/outbound/push/push.ts exports 'abstract class INotificationPushPort' with abstract send() method.

expectations.text: fcm-adapter-implements-port: FcmPushAdapter extends/implements the push port abstract class
expectations.evidence: fcm-push-adapter.ts: 'export class FcmPushAdapter implements INotificationPushPort'. Uses implements (valid TypeScript for abstract classes).

expectations.text: kafka-consumer-inbound: a Kafka consumer adapter exists under adapters/inbound/messaging/kafka/
expectations.evidence: File confirmed at src/adapters/inbound/messaging/kafka/notification-consumer.ts — correct hexagonal layer location.

expectations.text: kafka-consumer-separate-bootstrap: the consumer has its own bootstrap.ts separate from the Express HTTP bootstrap
expectations.evidence: src/adapters/inbound/messaging/kafka/bootstrap.ts exists as a separate composition root from src/adapters/inbound/http/express/bootstrap.ts.

expectations.text: kafka-topic-naming: Kafka topics follow notification.<past-tense-verb> convention
expectations.evidence: Topics confirmed: 'notification.created', 'notification.sent', 'notification.failed' — all follow <entity>.<past-tense-verb> convention.

expectations.text: consumer-no-business-logic: the Kafka consumer adapter only parses messages and delegates to use cases
expectations.evidence: notification-consumer.ts only parses JSON, switches on topic, and calls this.createNotification.execute(...). No domain logic inline.

expectations.text: redis-cache-ttl: the Redis cache adapter's set() call includes a TTL
expectations.evidence: N/A — Redis was not requested in this service's spec. No Redis adapter generated, which is correct behaviour.

expectations.text: config-zod-validated: infrastructure/config.ts uses a Zod schema to parse and validate environment variables
expectations.evidence: config.ts uses z.object() schema and schema.parse(). All process.env access isolated here.

expectations.text: js-extensions-on-imports: all local imports use the .js extension
expectations.evidence: grep found 33 files with .js imports and 0 local relative imports without .js extension.

expectations.text: test-doubles-extend-abstract-ports: doubles.ts uses 'extends' on the abstract port classes
expectations.evidence: doubles.ts: FakeWriteRepo extends INotificationsRepositoryWritePort, FakeReadRepo extends INotificationsRepositoryReadPort, FakeEventBus extends INotificationsEventBusPort, FakePushAdapter extends INotificationPushPort, PassthroughTelemetry extends INotificationsTelemetryPort.

expectations.text: e2e-uses-in-memory-adapters: _test/e2e/setup.ts wires the real controller with in-memory test doubles
expectations.evidence: setup.ts wires NotificationController with FakeWriteRepo, FakeReadRepo, FakeEventBus, FakePushAdapter, PassthroughTelemetry — no real DB or Kafka.

expectations.text: tdd-test-files-exist: test files exist for every layer
expectations.evidence: domain/_test/notification.test.ts, use-cases/_test/{doubles,create,get,mark-as-sent,mark-as-failed}.ts, _test/e2e/{setup,create,get,mark-as-sent,mark-as-failed}.ts all present.

expectations.text: no-process-env-outside-config: process.env is only accessed inside infrastructure/config.ts
expectations.evidence: grep found process.env only in src/infrastructure/config.ts. No other files.

expectations.text: graceful-shutdown-allsettled: Express bootstrap uses Promise.allSettled to close multiple connections on shutdown
expectations.evidence: bootstrap.ts lines 99-103: Promise.allSettled([postgresWriteConnection.close(), postgresReadConnection.close(), kafkaConnection.close()]).
